New Media Scotland · Alt-W Fund · 2016

Resono

Six extreme reverberant spaces across Scotland. Six compositions made for the acoustics within.

Scroll
The Concept

Every space has a sonic signature — a unique relationship between its geometry, materials and the sound within it. Resono sought to capture and compose for these signatures at their most extreme.

Impulse responses of six locations across Scotland were recorded and used as the compositional foundation: each piece of music was made in the studio with the real acoustics of the space loaded in, then taken back to the original location and re-recorded there.

The result is music that could only exist in that exact building, in that exact moment. Two recordings were also live events, with audiences inside the spaces.

The Method
01
Acoustic SurveyImpulse responses captured inside each space using a starter pistol, sine sweep or balloon burst. RT60 measured and documented.
02
Studio CompositionMusic composed in a controlled studio using convolution reverb loaded with the space's impulse response — hearing the real acoustics throughout.
03
Location Recording'Dry' recordings taken to the original spaces and re-recorded there — letting the acoustics respond naturally to the music made for them.
04
Release & ArchiveSelected recordings released as vinyl. Impulse responses archived and made available for use.
Featured on
BBC Radio 3 · Night Tracks BBC 6Music BBC Radio Scotland The Times

Blackford Hill Records · Vinyl
Cupar Grain Silo — Sam Annand

The Cupar Grain Silo recording, captured live in front of an audience inside the 200ft concrete structure, was released on vinyl by Blackford Hill Records. Featured on BBC Radio 3 Night Tracks and 6Music.
